skills/effective-web/references/fundamen.reason: Historical design and motion appendix; focused design, accessibility, and motion routes load smaller modules first.

skills/effective-web/references/layout-s.reason: Deep layout appendix retained for uncommon CSS patterns; the layout route selects a focused foundation or responsive module first.

skills/effective-web/references/tables-d.reason: Complete table cookbook retained for rare combined cases; normal table tasks select one focused concern.

skills/effective-web/references/navigati.reason: Pattern catalog retained for unusual navigation combinations; common structure and responsive decisions have focused modules.

skills/effective-web/references/print-we.reason: Long-form print cookbook retained for uncommon output constraints; normal print work loads the relevant artifact concern.

skills/effective-web/references/css-arch.reason: Architecture survey retained for migration and feature edge cases; the CSS route selects one implementation concern first.

skills/effective-web/references/scroll-p.reason: Comprehensive scroll catalog retained for specialist cases; navigation and feedback tasks have separate focused modules.

skills/effective-web/references/typograp.reason: Type survey retained for exceptional editorial and display treatments; routine system and text-detail work load smaller modules.

skills/effective-web/references/colour.m.reason: Full palette catalogue retained for advanced color work; contrast and token-system tasks load separate focused modules.

skills/effective-web/references/dialog-m.reason: Complete dialog catalog retained for unusual mobile and notification combinations; common implementation and product decisions are separate.

skills/effective-web/references/i18n-rtl.reason: Wide locale and RTL reference retained because correct use depends on the target locales; the route narrows the required sections.

skills/effective-web/references/seo.md.reason: Search implementation reference retained because checks differ materially by crawl, metadata, and structured-data task; the route narrows the required sections.

skills/effective-web/references/route-cs.reason: CSS is a broad router across cascade, tokens, organization, layout, and architecture; a task selects a focused module rather than loading every linked reference. Advisory only until the CSS route is split into narrower entry points.

skills/effective-web/references/route-de.reason: The design route spans research, visual system, motion, and accessibility appendices and is being restructured in parallel; registered without a ceiling so the in-flight content rework is not blocked by the advisory line budget.

skills/effective-web/references/route-pr.reason: Print work legitimately fans out into basics, content, and layout concerns; the route lists all three so a document task can pick the relevant one. Kept advisory rather than split because print requests often combine concerns.
